mnemonics: S.A.M.P.L.E. (Simple Attributes: Measuring Population Likelihood Estimates)
Think of sampling attributes like taking a 'SIP' from a big cup: Sample, Infer, Population.
keyPoints: Simple sampling of attributes involves drawing conclusions about a population proportion based on a sample.
The sample proportion (p-hat) is used as an unbiased estimator for the true population proportion (p).
The success-failure condition (np ≥ 10 and n(1-p) ≥ 10) must be checked to ensure the sampling distribution is approximately normal.
commonMistakes: Confusing the sample proportion (p-hat) with the true population proportion (p).
Forgetting to verify the success-failure condition before using the normal approximation for inference.
Using the standard deviation formula for the sampling distribution (sqrt(p(1-p)/n)) with the sample proportion (p-hat) instead of the assumed population proportion (p) when testing a hypothesis.
